notes: Roman consul noted for military command and desire for triumph; mentioned by Cicero and Cassius Dio.

docs.title: Letter I
docs.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
docs.excerpt: Most assuredly he is a thoroughly patriotic consul, and, as I always thought, an excellent fellow. Clodius does not beat about the bush, he is quite plainly aiming at the tribunate.

docs.title: Letter XXVI
docs.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
docs.excerpt: Need I say more? He is a truly patriotic consul and, as I have ever thought, naturally an honest man. That person, however, makes no disguise, but avowedly desires to be elected tribune.

docs.title: Correspondence, Book Volume 2
docs.author: Fronto / Marcus Aurelius
docs.excerpt: … Metellus proved himself a great and wise man no less than in the field, so just a mean did he keep between a pandering to popularity and undue severity . . . . and in a short time he restored the discipline of the army . [ 216 ] 11.
